title: Nonatelia rhizophylla
intro: Nonatelia rhizophylla, also known as the "Bitter Apple" or "Tobacco Root," is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Nonatelia rhizophylla include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as nonatelin, rhizophyllin, and apocynin. Nonatelia rhizophylla is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine as a tincture or powder, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
